meta.name: Orbit + Ink
meta.description: Design tokens for a rotating-habits app. One chromatic accent (coral / sand-coral). Calm, literary, warm.

target.styling: Unistyles 3 — StyleSheet.create(theme => …) with live adaptive light/dark
target.comment: This token map is authored for a native target. Names below match RN/Unistyles theme conventions. The web view of these tokens (CSS custom properties + @import fonts) lives in colors_and_type.css and is a secondary consumer — see legacyNames for the old web variable names.

legacyNames.comment: Old web-era token name → canonical name. The lab (index.html + *.jsx + colors_and_type.css) still uses the legacy CSS variable names (--bg, --ink, …); colors_and_type.css now also exposes the canonical names as aliases. New native ports should use the canonical names only.

text.comment: Near-black — primary text, primary CTA fill (was: ink)

accent.comment: Coral — single chromatic. Active rotation dot, NOW labels, kickers.

accentDim.comment: Coral pastel — outline of inactive dots, dividers.

accentSubtle.comment: Coral wash — soft background tint, swipe-to-tick pane (was: accentSoft).

shadow.comment: Express as a single RN boxShadow string per theme — Unistyles/RN 0.81+ accept the CSS-style shorthand. Used on all cards.
shadow._caveat: Keep the ambient layer's reach tight. Android's boxShadow renders as a plateau, not a true gaussian falloff — if the blur radius is large relative to the inter-card gap, the shadows of adjacent cards merge and flood the gap into a flat gray band. The 32px ambient blur below is tuned against the cozy card gap (12–16px); if you widen the blur, widen the gap too.

paused.comment: Coral-tinted glow on the paused banner only.

font.comment: Native target: embed discrete TTFs (expo-font / useFonts). fontFamily MUST equal each file's PostScript name — not 'Fraunces' + a weight prop. No Google Fonts @import, no variable-font axes (the opsz axis cannot be relied on across platforms — pick fixed sizes per role, see `type`).
font._shipped: We bundle exactly four faces: Fraunces-Regular, Fraunces-Italic, Inter-Regular, Inter-Medium. Any weight you reference but do not bundle renders as synthetic faux-bold on Android — do not assume Inter SemiBold/600 exists unless you add the file.
_caveats: Inter 600/SemiBold is intentionally NOT shipped (was a web dependency). Drop it or bundle Inter-SemiBold explicitly before using it.

Italic serif at small sizes (13–14px) silently drops trailing colon-digit runs (e.g. "9:09" loses the ":09") on Android — a HarfBuzz shaping bug with Fraunces-Italic. Never set clock times in italic; use Inter-Regular (caption) for times, or upright Fraunces.

serifItalic.comment: PostScript name. Group names, literary quotes. Avoid at <15px with colon-digit runs (Android).

sansRegular.comment: PostScript name. Meta, captions, body chrome.

type.comment: Fixed sizes per role (no opsz axis). `family` is a PostScript name from font.families.

heading.comment: Group / list-card names (was: groupDisplay). Italic-eligible via Fraunces-Italic.

label.comment: Kickers + section headers (was: kicker / sectionLabel). Section headers used 1.5 tracking — tighten to 1.5 if a label sits inline with body.

ringCenter.comment: Up-next text inside the orbit ring. Optional — no app equivalent in the current port (the ring center is empty); restore if you label the center.

buttonSmall.comment: Optional — no app equivalent. Use `button` at a smaller padding instead.

radius.comment: Flat T-shirt scale. Intent-per-size kept as comments, not as key names (the old hero/card/inner/subtle/input names collided with component names).

md.comment: Inputs blocks, secondary cards, queue list (was: inner)

spacing.comment: Single flat scale (replaces the old compact/cozy/roomy density model — RN apps don't carry a density mode). Values in px.

_usage.comment: The old 'cozy' defaults, expressed against this scale (cozy was the only canonical mode):
_usage.screenPaddingX: xl (20) — rounded from the old cozy 22
_usage.heroRingBox: top 2xl (24) · sides lg (16) · bottom xl (20) — from the old ringBoxPad '24px 14px 20px'
_usage.bottomSafeArea: 3xl (32) below the last content block (plus the device safe-area inset)

ring.comment: OrbitRing sizing. RN: build from Views + transforms (Reanimated) rather than SVG — see _impl. SVG remains valid on web.
ring._impl: Native recipe: lay dots out with absolute Views positioned by transform: [{rotate}, {translateY: -radius}, {rotate: '-…'}] so each dot stays upright; rotate the container View for the one-notch settle and counter-rotate dot content. Drive the 550ms settle + the halo pulse with Reanimated (useSharedValue + withTiming/withRepeat). SVG is optional and only worth it if you also draw the thin outer ring stroke.

outerRingStroke.comment: Thin outer ring stroke width (px). Web/SVG only; on native draw as a 1px bordered circular View or omit.

activeInnerDotRadius.comment: White punched-out dot inside the active coral dot

tickMarkOpacity.comment: Tick marks between dots are OPTIONAL — dropped in the current port. No progress arc, ever (the rotation loops forever).

ringRotate.comment: Headline rotation when a habit is ticked. RN: withTiming(target, { duration: 550, easing: Easing.bezier(.55,.06,.3,1) }).

settleHold.comment: Pause after rotation before cursor commits

haloPulse.comment: Active-dot halo: r 14→20→14, opacity .16→.06→.16. RN: withRepeat(withTiming(...), -1, true).

device.comment: Android Pixel-class, design canvas. Mobile-first. Real builds use the device safe-area insets, not these fixed numbers.
